senses.gloss: to grab, grasp, clutch, clasp, seize, take hold
to stick, cling, adhere, hang on
to have someone seize or hold something
recently cut hide of a new-born goat, scalp of a neonate

examples.text: يُسۡقَوۡنَ مِن رَّحِيقࣲ مَّخۡتُومٍ ۝ خِتَـٰمُهُۥ مِسۡكࣱۚ[…]
examples.english: They will be given to drink of full-flavoured sealed wine ۝ and its seal is of musk.

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *mašk-.

From Middle Persian [script needed] (mwšk' /⁠mušk⁠/, “musk”) (Persian مشک (mošk, “musk”)), from Sanskrit मुष्क (muṣka, “testicle, scrotum”).

Denominal verb of مِسْك (misk, “musk”).

Denominal verb of مُسْكَان (muskān, “retainer, earnest money”).
